purpose: Controlled vocabulary of SECULAR-DECLINE forces (the mirror image of disruptor_themes.json: these are the tails that destroy incumbent terminal value, not the winners). The Director tags each apex/value name with at most one PRIMARY secular_theme so the book is not concentrated in one melting tail. A theme carrying >=2 names must be size-capped via combined_caps (axis: 'secular-theme:<id>'); a WIDE non-eroding moat counts at HALF toward the theme's size budget (a durable franchise that merely carries the narrative is not the same risk as an eroding one).

concentration_rule.note: Aggregate size_units within a secular_theme, counting MOAT==WIDE & non-eroding names at 0.5. If the budget binds, emit a combined_caps {names, max_units, axis:'secular-theme:<id>'}; never let one secular tail carry the book.

themes.name: Generative-AI displacement of software & knowledge work
themes.thesis: LLMs/gen-AI collapse the marginal cost of 'good-enough' creative, code, research and advisory output, capping pricing power and ARPU for incumbents whose moat is workflow habit rather than hard lock-in.
victim_layers: horizontal SaaS with thin switching costs
themes.notes: The book's #1 concentration risk right now. A WIDE-moat net-cash compounder (ADBE-class: rising ROIC, expanding margin) carries this narrative but is NOT the same terminal risk as an eroding labor-arbitrage shop (GLOB-class: ROIC<WACC) — count the durable one at half.

themes.name: Stablecoin / wallet / fintech disintermediation of payments
themes.thesis: Stablecoin rails, digital wallets and Wise-style fintechs arbitrage the take-rate of cross-border transfer and the float of legacy processors toward zero, while cash/ATM usage structurally declines.
themes.notes: Watch for cohort de-rating (WU ~5x, Wise PBT guide down) — 'cheap vs peers' here is a sector-wide repricing, not idiosyncratic alpha. Often overlaps 'reimbursement-compression' for benefit-voucher names via regulation.

themes.name: Cord-cutting & broadband overbuild
themes.thesis: Streaming fragmentation drains linear video and advertising while fixed-wireless and fiber overbuild erode last-mile broadband pricing power — a regional inertia moat, not a switching-cost one.
themes.notes: Pricing power tells: ARPU concession + margin-funded unit defense. The cable cohort (Charter et al.) de-rates together.

themes.name: Autonomous-vehicle displacement of human mobility
themes.thesis: Robotaxi rollout (Waymo/Tesla) displaces human rideshare and trucking, impairing the terminal economics of subscale #2 operators first as AV operators route through their own 1P apps.
themes.notes: Diffusion is gradual but the lead market (SF) is the template — watch for measured share loss in launched cities, not the lagging national composite.

themes.name: AI automation of BPO / IT-services headcount
themes.thesis: AI decouples revenue from headcount in businesses whose economics are billing labor-hours at a spread, deflating bill-rates and compressing the arbitrage that funds the model.
themes.notes: Adjacent to ai-displacement; a name can carry BOTH (GLOB) and then counts toward both budgets. Margin-up while revenue-down is the late-cycle tell.

themes.name: Healthcare reimbursement & supplemental-payment compression
themes.thesis: Legislated cuts to Medicaid directed-payments, provider-tax safe-harbors and ACA subsidies unwind regulatory rent that props above-WACC returns at hospital and gov-health operators.
themes.notes: Dated (2028 glide) policy risk, NOT tech-secular — but it correlates the hospital cohort (THC/UHS/HCA) the same way. Already partly handled by the hospital-reimbursement exposure axis; this normalizes it.

themes.name: E-commerce / DTC channel shift
themes.thesis: Online and direct-to-consumer channels erode legacy brick-and-mortar retail and the wholesale distributors and mall-based formats that depended on physical footfall.
themes.notes: Not currently a seated tail; included so the Director can tag a name if one enters. Often pairs with demand-cycle beta in a recession.

themes.name: Fossil / legacy-energy demand displacement
themes.thesis: Electrification and renewables structurally displace demand for fossil fuels and legacy ICE-linked components over the long horizon, capping the terminal value of carbon-exposed incumbents.
themes.notes: Inverse of the disruptor 'energy_transition' theme. A policy-protected niche (e.g. CdTe solar inside a trade wall) is NOT a loser — judge on terminal demand, not the headline.
